rulings.Case Title: LUIS GONZALEZ VS. ANDREA DEBERRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 19, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT ANDREA DEBERRY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied because defendant fails to shift the burden of production. Defendant fails to demonstrate that plaintiff was covered by the cited insurance policy as a "residence employee" within the meaning of the policy. In addition, plaintiff obtained worker's compensation benefits under the Uninsured Employer Fund and defendant does show that Allstate's contribution was pursuant to the policy. The parties' compromise and release indicate that the worker's compensation proceeding did not resolve the instant action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: NAVEEN SINGHA et al VS. NISSIM LANYADOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 19, 2012, Line 8. DEFENDANT NISSIM LANYADOO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the loan was exempt from the interest rate limitations of Cal. Const. Article XV, §1. Specifically, there is a disputed issue regarding whether Mr. Campbell "arranged" the loan within the meaning of Civil Code § 1916.1. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EMILY LIOU et al VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 19, 2012, LINE 3, PLAINTIFFS EMILY LIOU AND SHYSHENQ LIOU'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Tentative Ruling: GRANTED. Vivian Li-Huey Ma Liou was deposed once already by Defendant Anthem Blue Cross. Anthem argues that CCP Section 2025.610(c)(1) permits a subsequent deposition of Plaintiff Vivien Liou because she was deposed on October 12, 2011 in her capacity as guardian ad litem for Emily Liou. Section 2025.610(c)(1) states that a subsequent deposition of a natural person who has previously been examined is allowed when, "(1) The person was examined as a result of that person's designation to testify on behalf of an organization under Section 2025.230." [emphasis added]. Even if Vivian Liou was deposed in a representative capacity as the guardian ad litem of Plaintiff Emily Liou, she nonetheless was not deposed on that date as a result of a designation to testify "on behalf of an organization under Section 2025.230." Ms. Emily Liou is not an "organization." She is, according to Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint, an incompetent adult. The Deposition Subpoena is hereby QUASHED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY KIN MING CHAN VS. JASON D TAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 19, 2012, Line 20. CROSS DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the first and sixth causes of action. Sustained with leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action. The demurrer to the first and sixth causes of action were not opposed. From the face of the cross complaint, the second and third causes of action are not clearly and affirmatively time-barred. The second and third causes of action, however, do not allege facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for equitable indemnity. Cross-Complainant is given ten days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for total and comparative equitable indemnity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EQUABLE ASCENT FINANCIAL LLC VS. CELINA GONZALEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Celina Gonzalez' Motion For Order To Compel Responses
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 19, 2012, LINE 4, Defendant Celina Gonzalez' Motion For Order To Compel Responses. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Celina Gonzalez's motion to compel Plaintiff to produce documents responsive to Defendant's Inspection Demand (Set One) and to compel Plaintiff to respond to Defendant's Special Interrogatories (Set One) is GRANTED. Plaintiff's failure to timely respond to the discovery propounded has waived all objections, including objections based upon attorney-client privilege and attorney work product. Plaintiff has not opposed Defendant's motion. Defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $300.00 is GRANTED. Within ten days of service of notice of entry of this order upon Plaintff: (1) Plaintiff shall provide verified responses without objection and produce all documents responsive Defendant's Inspection Demand (Set One) ; (2) Plaintiff shall provide verified responses without objection to Defendant's Special Interrogatories (Set One); and (3) Plaintiff shall pay Defendant $300.00 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SPECK CAB CO., INC., DBA ARROW CHECKER CAB, A et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 19, 2012, Line 29. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND CHRISTIANE HAYASHI'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The Court denies the anti-SLAPP motion as moot because plaintiff dismissed the action. The Court concludes that the complaint is a SLAPP and awards $5,637.50 in fees to defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
